title: Expressions to find surface area
description: Determine the expression that finds surface area of a 3D shape by adding the area of each face.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right triangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the prism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the triangular prism.
We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding the area of the triangles.

$\greenD{\text{Triangles}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \dfrac12\times \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times3\times4
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times12
\\\\
&= \greenD6 \end{align}$

There are two triangle faces with an area of $\greenD6$ square units.  So, we add $\greenD6$ twice.
 
Next, let's find the area of each rectangle.

$\blueD{\text{Left rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 3\times11
\\\\
&= \blueD{33} \end{align}$

$\purpleD{\text{Center rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 4\times11
\\\\
&= \purpleD{44} \end{align}$

$\maroonD{\text{Right rectangle}}$:  
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 5\times11
\\\\
&= \maroonD{55} \end{align}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the triangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\greenD6+\greenD6+\blueD{33}+\purpleD{44}+\maroonD{55}$

options.alt: A right triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 4 units and 11 units. The side face has side lengths of 3 units and 11 units. The diagonal side face has side lengths of 5 units and 11 units. The triangular prism has a height of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right triangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the prism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the triangular prism.
We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding area of the triangles.

$\greenD{\text{Triangles}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \dfrac12\times \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times4\times3
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times12
\\\\
&= \greenD{6} \end{align}$

There are two triangle faces with an area of $\greenD{6}$ square units.  So, we add $\greenD{6}$ twice.
 
Next, let's find the area of each rectangle.

$\blueD{\text{Left rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 3\times10
\\\\
&= \blueD{30} \end{align}$

$\purpleD{\text{Center rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 4\times10
\\\\
&= \purpleD{40} \end{align}$

$\maroonD{\text{Right rectangle}}$:  
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 5\times10
\\\\
&= \maroonD{50} \end{align}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the triangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\greenD{6}+\greenD{6}+\blueD{30}+\purpleD{40}+\maroonD{50}$

options.alt: A right triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 4 units and 10 units. The side face has side lengths of 3 units and 10 units. The diagonal side face has side lengths of 5 units and 10 units. The triangular prism has a height of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right triangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the prism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the triangular prism.
We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding area of the triangles.

$\greenD{\text{Triangles}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \dfrac12\times \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times6\times8
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times48
\\\\
&= \greenD{24} \end{align}$

There are two triangle faces with an area of $\greenD{24}$ square units.  So, we add $\greenD{24}$ twice.
 
Next, let's find the area of each rectangle.

$\blueD{\text{Left rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 6\times20
\\\\
&= \blueD{120} \end{align}$

$\purpleD{\text{Center rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 8\times20
\\\\
&= \purpleD{160} \end{align}$

$\maroonD{\text{Right rectangle}}$:  
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 10\times20
\\\\
&= \maroonD{200} \end{align}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.

$\greenD{24}+\greenD{24}+\blueD{120}+\purpleD{160}+\maroonD{200}$

The following expression represents the surface area of the triangular prism.

$2\cdot\greenD{24}+\blueD{120}+\purpleD{160}+\maroonD{200}$

options.alt: A right triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 8 units and 20 units. The side face has side lengths of 6 units and 20 units. The diagonal side face has side lengths of 10 units and 20 units. The triangular prism has a height of 6 units.

$\blueD{\text{Triangles}}$:

\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times8\times2
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times16
\\\\

Next, let's find the area of each rectangle.

$\maroonD{\text{Left rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 5\times11
\\\\

$\greenD{\text{Center rectangle}}$:

\\\\
 &= 8\times11
\\\\
&= \greenD{88} \end{align}$

$\maroonD{\text{Right rectangle}}$:

\\\\
 &= 5\times11
\\\\
&= \maroonD{55} \end{align}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  

$\blueD{8}+\blueD{8}+\maroonD{55}+\maroonD{55}+\greenD{88}$

options.alt: A triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 8 units and 11 units. The other 2 faces have side lengths of 5 units and 11 units. The triangular prism has a height of 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right triangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the prism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the triangular prism.
We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding area of the triangles.

$\blueD{\text{Triangles}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \dfrac12\times \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times6\times1
\\\\
 &= \dfrac12 \times6
\\\\
&= \blueD{3} \end{align}$

There are two triangle faces with an area of $\blueD{3}$ square units.  So, we add $\blueD{3}$ twice.
 
Next, let's find the area of each rectangle.

$\maroonD{\text{Left rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 4\times2
\\\\
&= \maroonD{8} \end{align}$

$\greenD{\text{Center rectangle}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 6\times2
\\\\
&= \greenD{12} \end{align}$

$\maroonD{\text{Right rectangle}}$:  
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 4\times2
\\\\
&= \maroonD{8} \end{align}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the triangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\blueD{3}+\blueD{3}+\greenD{12}+\maroonD{8}+\maroonD{8}$

options.alt: A triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 6 units and 2 units. The other 2 faces have side lengths of 4 units and 2 units. The triangular prism has a height of 1 unit.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the prism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the rectangular prism.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding the area of the bottom face.

The $\purpleE{\text{bottom rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &=  2\times4
\\\\
&= \purpleE{8} \end{align}$

The top face has the same area as the bottom face. So we add $\purpleE{8}$ twice.

Next, let's find the area of each other face.

The $\blueE{\text{front rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 4\times1
\\\\
&= \blueE{4} \end{align}$

The back face has the same area as the front face. So we add $\blueE{4}$ twice.

$\maroonE{\text{Leftmost rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 2\times1
\\\\
&= \maroonD{2} \end{align}$

The rightmost face has the same area as the leftmost face.  So, we add $\maroonE{2}$ twice.

Finally, we add the area of all $6$ faces.  We can find the surface area of the rectangular prism with the following expression:

$\purpleE8+\purpleE8+\blueE{4}+\blueE{4}+\maroonE{2}+\maroonE{2}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 4 units. The width is 2 units. The height is 1 unit.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right rectangular prism.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the rectangular prism.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding the area of the bottom face.

The $\purpleE{\text{bottom rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &=  3\times5
\\\\
&= \purpleE{15} \end{align}$

The top face has the same area as the bottom face. So we add $\purpleE{15}$ twice.

Next, let's find the area of each other face.

The $\blueE{\text{front rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 2\times5
\\\\
&= \blueE{10} \end{align}$

The back face has the same area as the front face. So we add $\blueE{10}$ twice.

$\maroonE{\text{Leftmost rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 2\times3
\\\\
&= \maroonE{6} \end{align}$

The rightmost face has the same area as the leftmost face.  So, we add $\maroonE{6}$ twice.

Finally, we add the area of all $6$ faces.  We can find the surface area of the rectangular prism with the following expression:

$\purpleE{15}+\purpleE{15}+\blueE{10}+\blueE{10}+\maroonE{6}+\maroonE{6}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 5 units. The width is 3 units. The height is 2 units.

\\\\
 &= 6\times3

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the prism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the rectangular prism.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

\\\\
 &=  2\times3
\\\\
&= \purpleE{6} \end{align}$

The top face has the same area as the bottom face. So we add $\purpleE{6}$ twice.

Next, let's find the area of each blue and red face.

$\blueE{\text{Leftmost rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 2\times5
\\\\
&= \blueE{10} \end{align}$

The rightmost face has the same area as the leftmost face.  So, we add $\blueE{10}$ twice.

The $\maroonE{\text{front rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height} 
\\\\
 &= 5\times3
\\\\
&= \maroonE{15} \end{align}$

The back face has the same area as the front face. So we add $\maroonD{15}$ twice.

Finally, we add the area of all $6$ faces.

$\purpleE{6}+\purpleE{6}+\blueE{10}+\blueE{10}+\maroonE{15}+\maroonE{15}$

The following expression represents the surface area of the rectangular prism.

$2\cdot \purpleE{6}+2 \cdot \blueE{10}+2 \cdot \maroonE{15}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 3 units. The width is 2 units. The height is 5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a right rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the prism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the rectangular prism.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding the area of the leftmost rectangular face of the net.

$\blueE{\text{Leftmost rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height}
\\\\
&= 7\times 8
\\\\
&= \blueE{56} \end{align}$

The rightmost face has the same area as the leftmost face.  So, we add $\blueE{56}$ twice.

Next, let's find the area of each of the other faces.

The $\goldE{\text{bottom rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height}
\\\\
&= 4\times 8
\\\\
&= \goldE{32} \end{align}$

The top face has the same area as the bottom face. So we add $\goldE{32}$ twice.

The $\maroonD{\text{front rectangular}}$ face:

$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{base}\times\text{height}
\\\\
&= 4\times 7
\\\\
&= \maroonD{28} \end{align}$

The back face has the same area as the front face. So we add $\maroonD{28}$ twice.

Finally, we add the area of all $6$ faces.

$\blueE{56}+\blueE{56}+\goldE{32}+\goldE{32}+\maroonD{28}+\maroonD{28}$

The following expression represents the surface area of the rectangular prism.

$2\cdot \blueE{56}+2 \cdot \goldE{32}+2 \cdot \maroonD{28}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 4 units. The width is 8 units. The height is 7 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a square-based pyramid.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the pyramid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the square pyramid.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

![Square pyramid net](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4ecfe3acf6496c20998e6cc58e02bbfc7d1847ca)

Let's start by finding the area of the square base.

$\maroonD{\text{Square}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{side}\times\text{side} 
\\\\
 &=  10\times10
\\\\
&= \maroonD{100}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of each of the triangular faces.

>$\begin{align} \blueD{\text{Area of a triangle}} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 10 \cdot 8\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 80 \\\\
&= \blueD{40} \\\\
\end{align}$

Four of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height. The total area of these four triangles is $\blueD{40}+\blueD{40}+\blueD{40}+\blueD{40}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the rectangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\maroonD{100}+\blueD{40}+\blueD{40}+\blueD{40}+\blueD{40}$

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 10 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 8 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a square-based pyramid.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the pyramid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the square pyramid.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

![Square pyramid net](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/09bcd16cd84cf899be580f550b0691a13c26bfe2)

Let's start by finding the area of the square base.

$\maroonD{\text{Square}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{side}\times\text{side} 
\\\\
 &=  6\times6
\\\\
&= \maroonD{36}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of each of the triangular faces.

>$\begin{align} \blueD{\text{Area of a triangle}} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 6 \cdot 5\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot  30\\\\
&= \blueD{15} \\\\
\end{align}$

Four of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height. The total area of these four triangles is $\blueD{15}+\blueD{15}+\blueD{15}+\blueD{15}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the rectangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\maroonD{36}+\blueD{15}+\blueD{15}+\blueD{15}+\blueD{15}$

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 6 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a square-based pyramid.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the pyramid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the square pyramid.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

![Square pyramid net](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/319db967be27cd6698b286db5e2c82701cbe9344)

Let's start by finding the area of the square base.

$\maroonD{\text{Square}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{side}\times\text{side} 
\\\\
 &=  4\times4
\\\\
&= \maroonD{16}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of each of the triangular faces.

>$\begin{align} \blueD{\text{Area of a triangle}} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot 3\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot  12\\\\
&= \blueD6 \\\\
\end{align}$

Four of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height. The total area of these four triangles is $\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the rectangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\maroonD{16}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}+\blueD{6}$

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 4 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a square-based pyramid.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the pyramid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the square pyramid.
We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.



[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding the area of the square base.

$\maroonD{\text{Square}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{side}\times\text{side} 
\\\\
 &=  2\times2
\\\\
&= \maroonD{4}\end{align}$
 
Next, let's find the area of each of the triangular faces.

>$\begin{align} \blueD{\text{Area of a triangle}} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 2 \cdot 3\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot  6\\\\
&= \blueD3 \\\\
\end{align}$

Four of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height. The total area of these four triangles is $\blueD{3}+\blueD{3}+\blueD{3}+\blueD{3}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the rectangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\maroonD{4}+\blueD{3}+\blueD{3}+\blueD{3}+\blueD{3}$

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 2 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a square-based pyramid.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the pyramid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the square pyramid.
We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding the area of the square base.

$\maroonD{\text{Square}}$:
$\begin{align} \text{Area} &= \text{side}\times\text{side} 
\\\\
 &=  4\times4
\\\\
&= \maroonD{16}\end{align}$
 
Next, let's find the area of each of the triangular faces.

>$\begin{align} \blueD{\text{Area of a triangle}} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot 5\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot  20\\\\
&= \blueD{10} \\\\
\end{align}$

Four of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height. The total area of these four triangles is $\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}$

Finally, we add the area of all $5$ faces.  The surface area of the rectangular prism can be found with the following expression:

$\maroonD{16}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}$

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 4 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following figure shows a cube.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expression represents the surface area of the cube?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Surface area** is a 2D measurement. It tells us the number of *square units* it would take to cover the faces of the  3D figure. 

[[☃ explanation 1]]

To find the surface area, we need to add the area of each face on the cube.

We can look at a net of the shape to find the area of each face.

[[☃ image 1]]

Let's start by finding the area of each face.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area of a square} &= \text{side}\times\text{side} 
\\\\
 &=  5\times 5
\\\\
&= 25
\end{align}$

There are six square faces with an area of $25$ square units each.   The following expression represents the surface area of the cube.

$25+25+25+25+25+25$

options.alt: A cube. The height is 5 units.
